trans-unit.source: Data preparation
trans-unit.target: Data preparation

trans-unit.source: First, we need to load the dataset into QGIS:
trans-unit.target: First, we need to load the dataset into QGIS:

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Import the data
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Import the data

trans-unit.source: In the top menu, go to **Layer → Add Layer → Add Vector Layer**
trans-unit.target: In the top menu, go to **Layer → Add Layer → Add Vector Layer**

trans-unit.source: Next to the **File name** field, click the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and browse to your **Som_outbreak_epi** file and click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: Next to the **File name** field, click the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and browse to your **Som_outbreak_epi** file and click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: Click **Add**. The layer will now appear in your **Layers** panel and the polygons will display on the map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Click **Add**. The layer will now appear in your **Layers** panel and the polygons will display on the map canvas.

trans-unit.source: Creating a map visualising weekly infections and infection trends
trans-unit.target: Creating a map visualising weekly infections and infection trends

trans-unit.source: As we want to visualize multiple variables later we choose to use a secondary point layer to map weekly infections. We will use week 8 of the outbreak for our example map. The number of new weekly infections (`week8_new_inf`) will control the **size** of each circle.
trans-unit.target: As we want to visualize multiple variables later we choose to use a secondary point layer to map weekly infections. We will use week 8 of the outbreak for our example map. The number of new weekly infections (`week8_new_inf`) will control the **size** of each circle.

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Create circles proportional to weekly infections
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Create circles proportional to weekly infections

trans-unit.source: **Create centroids for every region**
trans-unit.target: **Create centroids for every region**

trans-unit.source: Open the **Processing Toolbox Panel** and search for the `Centroids` tool and open it
trans-unit.target: Open the **Processing Toolbox Panel** and search for the `Centroids` tool and open it

trans-unit.source: As `Input Layer` select `Som_outbreak_epi`.
trans-unit.target: As `Input Layer` select `Som_outbreak_epi`.

trans-unit.source: Save the Centroids as `centroids_infections` to your drive by clicking "Run".
trans-unit.target: Save the Centroids as `centroids_infections` to your drive by clicking "Run".

trans-unit.source: Configuration of the centroid tool to generate centroids of the administrative district polygons :::
trans-unit.target: Configuration of the centroid tool to generate centroids of the administrative district polygons :::

trans-unit.source: These centroids take all fields and their stored information from the polygons they are created from, which is crucial if they are utilized for visualization of the stored variables.
trans-unit.target: These centroids take all fields and their stored information from the polygons they are created from, which is crucial if they are utilized for visualization of the stored variables.

trans-unit.source: **Visualize weekly infections with point size**
trans-unit.target: **Visualize weekly infections with point size**

trans-unit.source: Open the `Layer Styling Panel` select your centroid layer `Centroids_infections` and make sure the **Symbology** menue is selected.
trans-unit.target: Open the `Layer Styling Panel` select your centroid layer `Centroids_infections` and make sure the **Symbology** menue is selected.

trans-unit.source: At the top of the Symbology window, change the style from **Single Symbol** to **Graduated**
trans-unit.target: At the top of the Symbology window, change the style from **Single Symbol** to **Graduated**

trans-unit.source: As Value, choose `week8_inf_new`. This is the field that will control the size of each circle.
trans-unit.target: As Value, choose `week8_inf_new`. This is the field that will control the size of each circle.

trans-unit.source: Next to **Method**, change the default option **"Color"** to **Size**. This turns the graduated classification into a proportional circle map. Click "classify" and choose 5 classes for displaying you values.
trans-unit.target: Next to **Method**, change the default option **"Color"** to **Size**. This turns the graduated classification into a proportional circle map. Click "classify" and choose 5 classes for displaying you values.

trans-unit.source: As classification mode choose **"Equal Interval"** next to "Mode".
trans-unit.target: As classification mode choose **"Equal Interval"** next to "Mode".

trans-unit.source: Configuration of the Layer Styling Panel to achieve proportional point sizes :::
trans-unit.target: Configuration of the Layer Styling Panel to achieve proportional point sizes :::

trans-unit.source: :::{Tip} With the layer styling variable **"Size from ... to ..."** you can alter the size interval the points will cover to display the values. Try differnet options and choose which combination communicates the values optimally for you purpose. A good default for this visualization is 5 to 15 millimeters. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: :::{Tip} With the layer styling variable **"Size from ... to ..."** you can alter the size interval the points will cover to display the values. Try differnet options and choose which combination communicates the values optimally for you purpose. A good default for this visualization is 5 to 15 millimeters. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: To further enhance the information content of the map you can add labels to your circles that show the exact number of new infections for week 8.
trans-unit.target: To further enhance the information content of the map you can add labels to your circles that show the exact number of new infections for week 8.

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Add labels to weekly infections points
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Add labels to weekly infections points

trans-unit.source: Open the `Layer Styling Panel`, select your centroid layer `Centroids_infections` and select **Labels** in the side bar on the left hand side
trans-unit.target: Open the `Layer Styling Panel`, select your centroid layer `Centroids_infections` and select **Labels** in the side bar on the left hand side

trans-unit.source: In the labels panel select **"Single labels"** in the drop down menue.
trans-unit.target: In the labels panel select **"Single labels"** in the drop down menue.

trans-unit.source: As **"Value"** select the field containing the infection count for week 8 `week8_inf_new`.
trans-unit.target: As **"Value"** select the field containing the infection count for week 8 `week8_inf_new`.

trans-unit.source: Configuration of the Layer Styling Panel to generate point labels :::
trans-unit.target: Configuration of the Layer Styling Panel to generate point labels :::

trans-unit.source: Select the third panel of the label options and check the box **"Draw text buffer"** to generate a small buffer around you labels to enhance visibility.
trans-unit.target: Select the third panel of the label options and check the box **"Draw text buffer"** to generate a small buffer around you labels to enhance visibility.

trans-unit.source: To optimize the location of the point labels navigate to the **"Placement"** panel and for "Mode" select **"Offset from Point"**. With the **"Quadrant"** option you can determine the placement of the labels relative to the the centroid to you liking
trans-unit.target: To optimize the location of the point labels navigate to the **"Placement"** panel and for "Mode" select **"Offset from Point"**. With the **"Quadrant"** option you can determine the placement of the labels relative to the the centroid to you liking

trans-unit.source: Display the current infection trend with polygon colours
trans-unit.target: Display the current infection trend with polygon colours

trans-unit.source: Now that we have visualized our first epidemiological parameter ("weekly new cases") using proportional circle sizes, we can use our polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi` to display additional relevant information. Alongside the weekly infections, we want to show the **infection trend** from week 7 to week 8 for each regi to analyze, whether weekly infections are rising, falling, or stagnating. To achieve this, we first need to generate a new field using the **Field Calculator**:
trans-unit.target: Now that we have visualized our first epidemiological parameter ("weekly new cases") using proportional circle sizes, we can use our polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi` to display additional relevant information. Alongside the weekly infections, we want to show the **infection trend** from week 7 to week 8 for each regi to analyze, whether weekly infections are rising, falling, or stagnating. To achieve this, we first need to generate a new field using the **Field Calculator**:

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Generate a infection trend field with the field calculator
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Generate a infection trend field with the field calculator

trans-unit.source: **Open the field calculator**
trans-unit.target: **Open the field calculator**

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table of your polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi` by right clicking on it and select "Open Attribute Table" in the pop up window.
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table of your polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi` by right clicking on it and select "Open Attribute Table" in the pop up window.

trans-unit.source: In the attribute table open the `Field Calculator` by clicking on the ![](/fig/mod_4_fieldcalc_symbol.png) symbol at the top of the window.
trans-unit.target: In the attribute table open the `Field Calculator` by clicking on the ![](/fig/mod_4_fieldcalc_symbol.png) symbol at the top of the window.

trans-unit.source: **Create a new field diplaying infection trend**
trans-unit.target: **Create a new field diplaying infection trend**

trans-unit.source: In the Field Calculator interface keep "Create a new field" selected and choose `infection_trend` as "Output field name". As "Output field type" choose **"Text (string)"**
trans-unit.target: In the Field Calculator interface keep "Create a new field" selected and choose `infection_trend` as "Output field name". As "Output field type" choose **"Text (string)"**

trans-unit.source: In the "Expression" panel enter the following code chunk:
trans-unit.target: In the "Expression" panel enter the following code chunk:

trans-unit.source: For every polygon this code fills the newly generated field with "increase"/"decrease" if the week 8 value is higher/lower that the one of the previous week and with "no change" if both values are equal.
trans-unit.target: For every polygon this code fills the newly generated field with "increase"/"decrease" if the week 8 value is higher/lower that the one of the previous week and with "no change" if both values are equal.

trans-unit.source: Click on **"Ok"** to generated the new field.
trans-unit.target: Click on **"Ok"** to generated the new field.

trans-unit.source: Configuration of the Field Calculator interface to generate the infection_trend field :::
trans-unit.target: Configuration of the Field Calculator interface to generate the infection_trend field :::

trans-unit.source: With your newly generated infection trend field you are now able to display the trend direction for every region with polygon colour:
trans-unit.target: With your newly generated infection trend field you are now able to display the trend direction for every region with polygon colour:

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Vizualize the current infection trend
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Vizualize the current infection trend

trans-unit.source: Open the **Layer Styling Panel** and select your polygon layer layer `Som_outbreak_epi` and select **Symbology**.
trans-unit.target: Open the **Layer Styling Panel** and select your polygon layer layer `Som_outbreak_epi` and select **Symbology**.

trans-unit.source: At the top of the Symbology window, change the style from **Single Symbol** to **Categorized**
trans-unit.target: At the top of the Symbology window, change the style from **Single Symbol** to **Categorized**

trans-unit.source: As Value, choose `infection trend` to display the calculated trend direction.
trans-unit.target: As Value, choose `infection trend` to display the calculated trend direction.

trans-unit.source: Click "Classify" to execute the classification. Choose coulours of your liking for the values "decrease" and "increase" and "no change" (e.g. green, red and grey) by double clicking on the coloured squares in the panel displaying the classes.
trans-unit.target: Click "Classify" to execute the classification. Choose coulours of your liking for the values "decrease" and "increase" and "no change" (e.g. green, red and grey) by double clicking on the coloured squares in the panel displaying the classes.

trans-unit.source: As classification mode choose **"Equal Interval"** next to "Mode".
trans-unit.target: As classification mode choose **"Equal Interval"** next to "Mode".

trans-unit.source: For nicer visuals you can optionally also slightly reduce the opacity of the layer by adjusting it in the "Symbol" dropdown menue in the "Symbology" panel
trans-unit.target: For nicer visuals you can optionally also slightly reduce the opacity of the layer by adjusting it in the "Symbol" dropdown menue in the "Symbology" panel

trans-unit.source: Now display you polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi` together with you point layer `Centroids_infections` (centroids on top) to display all generated information in one map view. The result should look something like this (colour may vary):
trans-unit.target: Now display you polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi` together with you point layer `Centroids_infections` (centroids on top) to display all generated information in one map view. The result should look something like this (colour may vary):

trans-unit.source: If you want to generate a proper map layout of your epidemological overview map follow the descriptions outlined in [The Print Layout Composer](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_map_making_wiki.html).
trans-unit.target: If you want to generate a proper map layout of your epidemological overview map follow the descriptions outlined in [The Print Layout Composer](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_map_making_wiki.html).

trans-unit.source: Creating a map visualising weekly deaths and mortality rate
trans-unit.target: Creating a map visualising weekly deaths and mortality rate

trans-unit.source: After we created our multi variable map with information about the development of infections we can transfer the used steps to also create a map providing information about weekly deaths and the current mortality rate of the outbreak. The basic information needed is stored in the `weekX_dc` fields of your polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi`, which provide weekly death counts for every week of the cholera outbreak.
trans-unit.target: After we created our multi variable map with information about the development of infections we can transfer the used steps to also create a map providing information about weekly deaths and the current mortality rate of the outbreak. The basic information needed is stored in the `weekX_dc` fields of your polygon layer `Som_outbreak_epi`, which provide weekly death counts for every week of the cholera outbreak.

trans-unit.source: Create circles proportional to weekly infections
trans-unit.target: Create circles proportional to weekly infections

trans-unit.source: To create a point layer that visualizes weekly death count with proportional circles repeat the same steps that you learned previously when visualizing the new weekly infections. The only change you need to implement is changing the "Value" dropdwon menue in the Symbology panel from `week8_inf_new` to `week8_dc` to link the death count to the point sizes.
trans-unit.target: To create a point layer that visualizes weekly death count with proportional circles repeat the same steps that you learned previously when visualizing the new weekly infections. The only change you need to implement is changing the "Value" dropdwon menue in the Symbology panel from `week8_inf_new` to `week8_dc` to link the death count to the point sizes.

trans-unit.source: Display the current mortality rate with polygon colours
trans-unit.target: Display the current mortality rate with polygon colours

trans-unit.source: Alongside the weekly death count, we want to show the **mortality rate** from week 7 to week 8. To achieve this, we first need to generate a new field using the **Field Calculator**. beforehand make sure to create a duplicate layer of `Som_outbreak_epi` to dont overwrite you previous vizualisation. Call the dupolicate layer `Som_outbreak_mort`.
trans-unit.target: Alongside the weekly death count, we want to show the **mortality rate** from week 7 to week 8. To achieve this, we first need to generate a new field using the **Field Calculator**. beforehand make sure to create a duplicate layer of `Som_outbreak_epi` to dont overwrite you previous vizualisation. Call the dupolicate layer `Som_outbreak_mort`.

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Generate a moertality rate field with the field calculator
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Generate a moertality rate field with the field calculator

trans-unit.source: **Open the field calculator**
trans-unit.target: **Open the field calculator**

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table of your polygon layer `Som_outbreak_mort` by right clicking on it and select "Open Attribute Table" in the pop up window.
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table of your polygon layer `Som_outbreak_mort` by right clicking on it and select "Open Attribute Table" in the pop up window.

trans-unit.source: In the attribute table open the `Field Calculator` by clicking on the ![](/fig/mod_4_fieldcalc_symbol.png) symbol at the top of the window.
trans-unit.target: In the attribute table open the `Field Calculator` by clicking on the ![](/fig/mod_4_fieldcalc_symbol.png) symbol at the top of the window.

trans-unit.source: **Create a new field diplaying infection trend**
trans-unit.target: **Create a new field diplaying infection trend**

trans-unit.source: In the Field Calculator interface keep "Create a new field" selected and choose "mortality_rate" as "Output field name". As "Output field type" choose **"Decimal number (real)"**
trans-unit.target: In the Field Calculator interface keep "Create a new field" selected and choose "mortality_rate" as "Output field name". As "Output field type" choose **"Decimal number (real)"**

trans-unit.source: In the "Expression panel" enter the following code chunk:
trans-unit.target: In the "Expression panel" enter the following code chunk:

trans-unit.source: For every polygon this code divides the weekly death count of week 8 by the total infections and multiplies by 100 to calculate the mortality rate in percent. The resulting value is stored in the newly generated field `mortality_rate`
trans-unit.target: For every polygon this code divides the weekly death count of week 8 by the total infections and multiplies by 100 to calculate the mortality rate in percent. The resulting value is stored in the newly generated field `mortality_rate`

trans-unit.source: Click on **"Ok"** to generated the new field.
trans-unit.target: Click on **"Ok"** to generated the new field.

trans-unit.source: Configuration of the Field Calculator interface to generate the infection trend field ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Configuration of the Field Calculator interface to generate the infection trend field ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: With your newly generated moertality rate field you are now able to display the value for every region with polygon colour:
trans-unit.target: With your newly generated moertality rate field you are now able to display the value for every region with polygon colour:

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Vizualize the current mortality rate
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Vizualize the current mortality rate

trans-unit.source: Open the `Layer Styling Panel` and select your polygon layer layer `Som_outbreak_mort` and select **Symbology**.
trans-unit.target: Open the `Layer Styling Panel` and select your polygon layer layer `Som_outbreak_mort` and select **Symbology**.

trans-unit.source: At the top of the Symbology window, change the style from **Single Symbol** to **Graduated**
trans-unit.target: At the top of the Symbology window, change the style from **Single Symbol** to **Graduated**

trans-unit.source: As Value, choose `mortality_rate` to display the calculated percentage.
trans-unit.target: As Value, choose `mortality_rate` to display the calculated percentage.

trans-unit.source: Click "Classify" to execute the classification. Choose a colour palette of your liking, e.g. "Reds".
trans-unit.target: Click "Classify" to execute the classification. Choose a colour palette of your liking, e.g. "Reds".

trans-unit.source: As classification mode choose **"Equal Interval"** next to "Mode".
trans-unit.target: As classification mode choose **"Equal Interval"** next to "Mode".

trans-unit.source: For nicer visuals you can optionally also slightly reduce the **opacity** of the layer by adjusting it in the "Symbol" dropdown menue in the "Symbology" panel
trans-unit.target: For nicer visuals you can optionally also slightly reduce the **opacity** of the layer by adjusting it in the "Symbol" dropdown menue in the "Symbology" panel

trans-unit.source: Display both the current death count and mortality rate layers together to get an overview of the situation. The result should look something like this:
trans-unit.target: Display both the current death count and mortality rate layers together to get an overview of the situation. The result should look something like this:
